emotional_feel: Trustworthy local expert with Southern warmth. The site should feel like calling a reliable neighbor who happens to be an HVAC pro — approachable, competent, and reassuring. Charleston charm meets technical confidence. The founder started from his truck in 2014 and built a 30+ person team — that hustle and care should come through.
customer_pain: Charleston summers are brutal — when AC breaks, it's an emergency. Customers are stressed, sweating, and worried about being overcharged. They need fast response, transparent pricing, and confidence that the job will be done right the first time. Many are also dealing with aging systems and dreading the cost of replacement.
trust_triggers: In business since 2014 — 10+ years serving Charleston
150+ years combined HVAC experience on the team
Locally owned — founder Marshall Gay started from his truck
current_weaknesses: Hero section lacks emotional punch — generic stock photo with text overlay, doesn't feel Charleston-specific
Phone number isn't prominent enough in mobile view — needs sticky header CTA
Reviews/social proof not visible above fold — trust signals buried below
Too many navigation items create decision paralysis — 9 sub-menus under AC alone
Promotions page has expired offers mixed with current ones — looks unmanaged
No team photos or faces — feels corporate despite being a local family business
Copy is functional but lacks personality — doesn't tell the founder's story effectively
Service pages feel templated — same layout for every service
Color palette (green/gray) is fine but underutilized — site feels visually flat
No maintenance plan page (404) — missed recurring revenue opportunity
wow_moment: Trust ribbon + stats counter section — animated counters showing '10+ Years Serving Charleston', '30+ Team Members', '15 Service Vehicles', '150+ Years Combined Experience', paired with the Google Guaranteed badge and A+ BBB rating. This section will be immediately visible and create instant credibility that their current site buries. Secondary wow: a Charleston-specific hero with emotional copy about keeping Lowcountry families comfortable.
